steps.rationale: Keeping whisper but shifting the generator toward a stricter SOAP structure with stronger preprocessing should test whether the main issue is formatting discipline and cleanup rather than the ASR backbone.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves the main clinical picture and core assessment, but it falls short of the expected note in several clinically important areas. Key omissions include the penicillin allergy, several vitals, and the explicit return precautions; the plan is also noticeably more generic. In addition, awkward phrasing and meta-commentary about transcript drift and comparison to a golden note make the document less professional and less chart-ready.

steps.rationale: This variant isolates ASR impact with a lighter prompt change, aiming to preserve rich detail while reducing omissions from fragmented recognition through parakeet plus slightly stricter turn detection.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact captures the broad clinical picture of a likely viral upper respiratory infection, but it falls short of the expected note because it omits multiple important history, vital, exam, and plan details, and it fails to document explicit return precautions. The SOAP structure helps readability, but unsupported transcript fragments and meta commentary make the note feel less professional and less chart-ready than the target.

steps.rationale: Switching ASR to parakeet while tightening cleanup and using a SOAP-oriented prompt directly targets transcript leakage, fragmented multilingual artifacts, and weak section completeness.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves only part of the clinical picture and misses too many specific facts, especially medications, allergy, full vitals/exam details, and nearly all concrete return precautions. Its basic SOAP structure helps, but the note is weakened by omissions, vague planning, and unprofessional transcript leakage, so it falls well short of the expected follow-up note.
